dna.description: Criteo is a global commerce media company that enables marketers and media owners to drive better commerce outcomes through its Commerce Media Platform. Leveraging AI and the world's largest open commerce dataset, Criteo delivers personalized advertising across the open internet, helping brands acquire, retain, and re-engage customers. Listed on NASDAQ (CRTO), Criteo serves over 22,000 advertisers worldwide.
products: Commerce Media Platform (unified demand-side and supply-side solution)
Commerce Growth (customer acquisition and retention for brands)
Commerce Max (self-service DSP for commerce audiences)
Commerce Grid (SSP for retailer and publisher monetization)
Commerce Yield (monetization for retail media networks)
Criteo AI Engine (machine learning-powered bidding and optimization)
Retail Media (on-site and off-site advertising for retailers)
features: Real-time bidding across open internet inventory
Cross-device identity resolution without third-party cookies
use_cases: Retail media monetization for e-commerce platforms
First-party data activation for personalized ads
Video and connected TV commerce advertising
In-store traffic attribution from digital campaigns
technologies: Hadoop / Spark for big data processing
dna.pricing_model: Performance-based pricing (cost-per-click or cost-per-acquisition) for advertisers. SaaS licensing fees for Commerce Yield and Commerce Grid products. Managed service fees for enterprise accounts.
dna.company_size_signals: 3,500+ employees, 22,000+ advertisers, revenue of USD 1.9 billion (2024). Headquartered in Paris, France. Listed on NASDAQ (CRTO).
social_proof: Fnac Darty (retail media and performance advertising)
Carrefour (retail media network powered by Criteo)
dna.tone_of_voice: Data-driven, performance-oriented, innovative, with emphasis on open internet commerce and measurable outcomes

market.market_size_estimate: Global digital advertising market: ~USD 680 billion (2024). Commerce media / retail media: ~USD 55 billion. Programmatic advertising: ~USD 200 billion. Total addressable market for Criteo: ~USD 120 billion.
market.growth_trend: Growing at 12-15% CAGR driven by retail media network expansion, deprecation of third-party cookies pushing demand for first-party data solutions, and growth of commerce media as a category. Retail media is the fastest-growing segment at 25%+ CAGR.
likely_competitors: The Trade Desk (leading independent DSP for programmatic advertising)
Google DV360 (dominant advertising platform with deep search and YouTube integration)
Meta Ads (walled garden with massive social commerce reach)
Amazon Advertising (largest retail media network globally)
CitrusAd / Epsilon (retail media solutions acquired by Publicis)
PromoteIQ / Microsoft Advertising (retail media and commerce solutions)
competitive_advantages: Largest open commerce dataset with 35 billion daily browsing and buying events
Deep AI expertise with 18+ years of machine learning in advertising
Dual-sided platform serving both advertisers and media owners
Strong position in retail media enablement
First-party data-centric approach for post-cookie world
Global scale with direct integrations to 22,000+ advertisers
market_challenges: Third-party cookie deprecation requires fundamental technology shifts
Google and Meta dominate digital ad budgets as walled gardens
Amazon building dominant position in retail media
Increasing privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy) restricting data usage
Customer concentration risk with large e-commerce clients
customer_pain_points: Declining return on ad spend from walled garden platforms
Inability to measure true commerce outcomes across channels
Third-party cookie loss breaking retargeting and attribution
Fragmented retail media landscape with no unified buying platform
Lack of transparency in programmatic advertising supply chain
Difficulty connecting online advertising to in-store purchases
buying_triggers: Launch of e-commerce channel or marketplace
Third-party cookie deprecation forcing new advertising strategies
Retailer launching or scaling a retail media network
Need to reduce dependence on Google and Meta ad platforms
Desire to activate first-party commerce data for advertising
Seasonal commerce events (Black Friday, holiday season)
Merger or acquisition of retail brands requiring unified ad strategy
market.sales_cycle_estimate: 2-6 weeks for self-service performance advertising, 2-4 months for enterprise Commerce Media Platform deployments, 4-8 months for retail media network implementations
ideal_customer_characteristics: E-commerce brand with monthly website traffic above 500,000 visits
Annual digital advertising budget above USD 500,000
Retailer with first-party transaction data from loyalty programs
Company selling products across multiple online channels
Brand looking to diversify away from walled garden ad platforms
Retailer building or scaling a retail media network
Company with strong first-party data assets seeking monetization
Multi-brand enterprise needing unified commerce advertising
Business with measurable online-to-offline commerce journeys
Organization investing in data-driven marketing technology
anti_icp_signals: Small business with fewer than 10,000 monthly website visitors (insufficient data for AI optimization)
Company with no e-commerce or digital commerce presence
Brand exclusively advertising within walled gardens with no open internet strategy
Organization with no first-party data collection capability
Business in a highly restricted advertising vertical (tobacco, weapons)
Startup with no established customer base or transaction history
Company opposed to programmatic or data-driven advertising
industry_trends: Retail media networks becoming third major ad channel after search and social
AI and machine learning automating campaign optimization
Convergence of brand and performance advertising
Commerce media extending beyond online to in-store and CTV
First-party data becoming the new currency of advertising

clients.tech_stack: Cloud;Commerce Media Platform;Retail Media;Commerce Yield;Commerce Grid
